because it is effective to abolish the coughing reflex, which is not wanted during the procedure and might be dangerous in the non-inhibited method.

There are two branches of the apical posterior division of the left upper pulmonary artery.

After isolation and the divation of the apical posterior branches of the left upper pulmonary artery and the veins, we kept going on to the upper divation of the left upper bronchus.
After ligation of the targeted bronchus, we proceed to the separation of the upper posterior segment from the lingual lobe, which is intended to be preserved.
After the apical posterior segment was separated, we inserted a protective bag to remove the tumor via the protective bag.
Then the lymph node station and dissection was performed to obtain lymph nodes for further cancer staging.
